highlight.title: GitHub Actions Agentic Workflows Hit Technical Preview
highlight.summary: <cite index="17-4,17-5,17-6">GitHub has launched Agentic Workflows that let developers write CI/CD automation in plain Markdown instead of YAML. The feature converts natural language descriptions into standard GitHub Actions workflows using AI agents.</cite>

resources.title: Claude Code Dominates Terminal-Based AI Development
resources.why_now: <cite index="3-14,3-15">Claude Code has emerged as the undisputed leader for CLI-based agentic coding, with Opus 4.6 handling complex multi-file tasks better than competitors.</cite> <cite index="1-18,1-19">The AI development tool landscape is converging around distinct architectures where workflow preference matters more than benchmark scores.</cite>

resources.title: ReSharper Extension Now Available for VS Code & Cursor
resources.why_now: <cite index="21-2,21-3">JetBrains officially released ReSharper for VS Code and compatible editors after a year in preview, bringing professional C# code analysis to AI-first environments like Cursor.</cite> <cite index="21-7">The extension remains free for non-commercial use while targeting the growing developer preference for lightweight editors.</cite>

resources.title: GitHub Actions Gets Major Security & Workflow Upgrades
resources.why_now: <cite index="11-1,11-2">GitHub's 2026 roadmap shifts Actions toward secure-by-default automation in response to increasing supply chain attacks targeting CI/CD systems.</cite> <cite index="12-7,12-8">New features like OIDC custom properties and service container overrides address long-standing developer frustrations while hardening security.</cite>

resources.title: Zed Editor Launches Agent Client Protocol with JetBrains
resources.why_now: <cite index="25-13,25-14">Zed announced the Agent Client Protocol in January 2026, enabling external AI agents like Claude Code and Codex CLI to work inside Zed without editor lock-in.</cite> <cite index="25-9,25-10">Built in Rust with sub-second startup times and 40% faster edit latency than VS Code, Zed represents the performance-first response to AI-heavy development workflows.</cite>

claude_take: 2026 is shaping up as the year AI coding tools mature beyond simple autocomplete into full workflow automation. The convergence around three architectural approaches—terminal-native (Claude Code), IDE-native (Cursor), and extension-native (Copilot)—suggests the market is stabilizing around distinct use cases rather than one-size-fits-all solutions.
